text.title: WBS Creation Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules grounding WBS Creation. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Every WBS node name MUST be a noun phrase (an output) — never a verb phrase (an activity). Reject names matching `^(Build|Design|Implement|Test|Code|Develop|Create) `.

rationale.source: PMI Practice Standard for Work Breakdown Structures (3rd ed.); PMBOK 7 Planning Performance Domain

rule.rationale: Deliverable names remain stable when implementation changes; activity names do not. "User Authentication Module" survives a rewrite; "Build user authentication" does not.

rule.statement: Every parent MUST represent exactly 100% of its children's scope — no gaps, no overlaps. Verified by `weight_pct` summing to 100 (+/- 1%) per parent.

rationale.source: PMI WBS Practice Standard; DoD MIL-STD-881F

rule.rationale: Gaps create forgotten work that surfaces late. Overlaps create double-counted effort and budget conflicts.

rule.statement: Leaf work packages MUST have an effort estimate in [8, 80] hours. Outliers MUST be split (> 80) or merged (< 8). Tracking unit may shift the bounds, but a calibrated range must exist.

rationale.source: PMI WBS Practice Standard; PRINCE2 product-based planning

rule.rationale: Below 8 hours, tracking overhead exceeds value. Above 80 hours, the estimate is a guess and dependencies are invisible.

rule.statement: The WBS MUST include all six overhead branches: Project Management, Quality Assurance, Deployment, Documentation, Training, Transition. Missing branches are flagged as a defect, NOT silently omitted.

rationale.source: PMI cost-management knowledge area; agency P&L cohort data

rule.rationale: Overhead is 15-25% of real project effort. Agents and manual drafters systematically omit it, creating a structural budget gap.

rule.statement: WBS numeric IDs (1, 1.1, 1.1.1) are append-only. Once assigned, an ID MUST NEVER change or be reused, even after node deletion. Renumbering is rejected.

rationale.source: PMI WBS Practice Standard ID-stability guidance

rule.rationale: Change requests, risk entries, and tasks reference WBS IDs. Renumbering breaks traceability across the entire project record.

rule.statement: Every leaf MUST have a Dictionary entry with all required fields: id, name, description (included AND excluded scope), deliverable, acceptance_criteria (observable + testable), owner (one accountable person or named team), effort_hours (8-80), dependencies (predecessor IDs).

rationale.source: PMI WBS Practice Standard Dictionary chapter; PMBOK Scope Management knowledge area

rule.rationale: Missing acceptance criteria make sign-off subjective. Missing exclusions cause scope creep. Missing ownership causes handoff gaps.
